this time last year: used to refer to the same time in the previous year
a piece of cake: something that is very easy to do
break a leg: a way to wish someone good luck, especially before a performance
hit the sack: to go to bed in order to sleep
let the cat out of the bag: to reveal a secret or disclose something that was supposed to be kept confidential
barking up the wrong tree: to pursue a mistaken or misguided course of action
the ball is in your court: it is now your responsibility to take action or make a decision
burn the midnight oil: to work late into the night
hit the nail on the head: to describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem
by the fact that: because of the fact that; due to the fact that
in the blink of an eye: very quickly; in a very short time
piece of cake: something that is very easy to do
deep sense of something: a strong feeling or understanding of something
deep sense of work: a strong feeling or understanding of work
deep sense of purpose: a strong feeling or understanding of one's purpose in life
in the long run: over a long period of time; eventually
in the short run: over a short period of time; temporarily
in the meantime: during the time between two events; meanwhile
in the nick of time: just in time; at the last possible moment
in the same boat: in the same situation; facing the same challenges
in the heat of the moment: in a moment of intense emotion or excitement
in the middle of nowhere: in a remote or isolated place
